hero_subtitle: SFTY helps government authorities preserve a secure, traceable audit trail across suspicious activity, case management, and reporting workflows to support defensible AML decisions.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Audit readiness weakens across disconnected logs
problem_description: Regulators face challenges when AML supervision data and investigation evidence remain scattered across multiple systems. This fragmentation makes it harder to trace case history, defend decisions, and prepare timely suspicious activity reports.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY centralizes AML data, links suspicious activity signals with case evidence, preserves comprehensive audit trails, and organizes reporting workflows so regulators can manage investigation-ready records with clear traceability.
workflow_title: From suspicious activity to audit-ready evidence
workflow_description: SFTY organizes suspicious activity signals through case creation, evidence preservation, supervisory review and reporting to support defensible government decision making.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One evidence-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented AML signals into structured cases, audit-ready evidence and supervisory workflows, enabling regulators to maintain defensible records and clear investigation trails.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY supports regulators with secure deployment, controlled access and evidence-grade logging to maintain defensible records for AML case management and FIU workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support audit-ready logs for AML supervision
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how suspicious activity evidence supports FIU workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how case management preserves audit trail integrity
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for government AML infrastructure
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML regulators?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides AML regulators with a supervisory layer connecting transaction data, licensing, KYC/KYB, Travel Rule and risk signals into structured workflows and audit-ready records.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure suspicious activity cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes signals from wallet activity, alerts and providers into cases with linked evidence, supervisory notes, decisions and reporting drafts to support FIU investigations.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting evidence with audit-grade logging to support transparency and defensible supervisory reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, reporting systems and analytics providers while maintaining secure access governance and phased deployment options.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current AML analytic tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY connects and structures outputs from existing AML tools without replacing analytics or compliance providers.
final_title: Review audit-ready evidence workflows with SFTY
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports defensible AML records, case management, supervisory logging and structured reporting for government teams.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
